metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2) requiring that the eval suite is run with the `[eval]` extra and the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer passes on `CV-two-unmet-criteria` (e.g., `pytest --extras eval -k CV-two-unmet-criteria` exits 0) so the behavioral proof has a concrete proving command in the acceptance gate.
findings.finding: AC2 requires only `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` (an offline schema/format check) as the proving command for the eval case, but the central behavioral claim — that the prompt actually emits two distinct findings for two unmet criteria — can only be proven by running the live eval (e.g., `pytest -m eval` or equivalent). A passing schema-validation command does not exercise the prompt or the scorer at all, so the behavioral proof has no AC-level proving command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'Offline `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to: 'Offline `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec; AND running the eval suite (`pytest --extras eval -k CV-two-unmet-criteria` or equivalent) exits 0 with the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer reporting pass, confirming the prompt emits >= 2 distinct findings for the two-gap fixture.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan's 'What' section and AC2: the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer must be implemented and registered in `REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` (e.g. asserting `len(findings) >= N` and that each finding names a distinct criterion), so that `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` and the existing `test_new_eval_specs` CI gate both pass.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 requires adding a deterministic scorer named `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` to `completion-verifier.eval.yaml` AND states that `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec — but `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` rejects any deterministic scorer not present in `REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` (confirmed: eval.py:311-317 loads `known_scorer_names()` and `validate_scorer` rejects unlisted names; `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` is absent from `REGISTRY`). The plan never mentions adding the scorer implementation to `eval_scorers.py`, so the stated AC2 self-consistency claim ('validate_eval_spec(strict=True) passes') rests on an unbacked assumption that the scorer name will be registered.
findings.location: AC2 / What section ('new deterministic scorer enumerates_all_unmet_criteria') / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py REGISTRY
findings.suggested_fix: Add a work item: 'Add `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` to `REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`. The scorer applies only to `expect: fail` cases that declare `min_findings: N` (or embed N in the case metadata); it passes when `len(out["findings"]) >= N` and the N criterion fields are pairwise distinct.' Then update AC2 to reference this file explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` as an explicit edit target and define the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer function there (registering it in `REGISTRY`), so `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec.
findings.finding: The plan requires `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to pass on the extended spec (AC item 2), but strict mode validates that every deterministic scorer name is registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py). The new `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer name is not currently in REGISTRY, and `eval_scorers.py` is not named as an edit target anywhere in the plan — so adding it only to the .eval.yaml will cause `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to fail.
findings.suggested_fix: In eval_scorers.py, implement a scorer function (e.g. `_enumerates_all_unmet_criteria`) that, given a `CV-two-unmet-criteria` case, checks that the output's `findings` list contains at least two entries each naming a distinct criterion. Register it in `REGISTRY` as `'enumerates_all_unmet_criteria': _enumerates_all_unmet_criteria`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add implementation of `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` to `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` (REGISTRY + scorer function) so `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` accepts it as a known deterministic scorer name, satisfying AC2.
findings.finding: The plan requires `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to pass (AC2) but never mentions implementing the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer in `eval_scorers.py`. Strict validation calls `known_scorer_names()` which returns `frozenset(REGISTRY)` — any deterministic scorer name absent from that registry is rejected. Adding the scorer name only to the YAML without a matching registry entry will cause strict validation to fail, breaking AC2.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to eval_scorers.py: a scorer function `_enumerates_all_unmet_criteria(case, out)` that (a) is only applicable when `case.get('expect') == 'fail'` and the case carries a `min_findings` or `criteria_count` field (e.g. 2 for CV-two-unmet-criteria), (b) counts distinct `criterion` values in `_findings(out)` and returns `ScoreResult(True, count >= expected_n)`. Then register it in REGISTRY as `'enumerates_all_unmet_criteria': _enumerates_all_unmet_criteria`. Also add `criteria_count: 2` to the CV-two-unmet-criteria dataset case so the scorer knows N=2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer implementation to `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` so that `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` accepts the extended spec (AC2 requires this but the plan never mentions it).
findings.finding: The plan adds a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` to completion-verifier.eval.yaml and asserts AC2 ('validate_eval_spec(strict=True) passes on the extended spec'), but validate_eval_spec(strict=True) rejects any deterministic scorer name not registered in eval_scorers.REGISTRY — and the plan never mentions implementing this scorer in eval_scorers.py.
findings.location: AC2 / What section ('Add to completion-verifier.eval.yaml … enumerates_all_unmet_criteria scorer') / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py REGISTRY
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'Register `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py): a scorer that, on a FAIL verdict, checks len(findings) >= N where N is the number of independently-unmet criteria in the dataset case (e.g. carried in a `min_findings` field per case). Without this, validate_eval_spec(strict=True) will reject the scorer name.' Also add a corresponding AC bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan's 'What' section (and mirror in AC line 2) that `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` must be implemented as an entry in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` — without this, `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` rejects the new scorer name and AC line 2 cannot pass.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` in completion-verifier.eval.yaml but never specifies adding it to the REGISTRY in eval_scorers.py — `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` (which AC line 2 requires to pass) will reject any deterministic scorer name absent from REGISTRY.
findings.location: What section / AC line 2 / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py REGISTRY (lines 370-401) / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py validate_scorer (line 129)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' bullet for the eval YAML change, add: 'Also register `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (eval_scorers.py) — strict validation rejects deterministic scorer names absent from the registry.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer implementation to `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`; without a registry entry, `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` rejects the scorer name and AC line 2 cannot pass.
findings.finding: The plan's new `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer name is referenced only in the eval YAML, but `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` — which AC line 2 requires to pass — rejects any deterministic scorer name not present in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY`. The scorer is absent from the registry; the plan never mentions adding it there.
findings.location: What section / AC line 2: 'Offline validate_eval_spec(strict=True) passes on the extended spec'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py REGISTRY
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scorer function to eval_scorers.py — e.g. `def _enumerates_all_unmet_criteria(case, out): findings = _findings(out); n = case.get('min_findings', 1); return ScoreResult(case.get('expect') in FIRE_EXPECTS, len(findings) >= n and len({f.get('criterion') for f in findings}) >= n)` — and register it under `'enumerates_all_unmet_criteria'` in REGISTRY, then reference `min_findings: 2` in the `CV-two-unmet-criteria` dataset case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC4, such as `git diff HEAD~1 -- src/rebar/llm/completion/reconcile_verdict.py` producing empty output (or `sha256sum` comparison), so byte-identity is mechanically verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'reconcile_verdict and the close-path logic are byte-unchanged' — a concrete, specific claim — but provides no proving command (e.g., a `git diff` assertion or hash check) to verify it. Without a command, a reviewer cannot distinguish 'unchanged' from 'accidentally touched during refactoring.'
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'reconcile_verdict and the close-path logic are byte-unchanged (no reconciliation/threshold edits).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Verified by `git diff HEAD -- src/rebar/llm/completion/reconcile_verdict.py` (and the close-path module) producing no output in the final PR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default discrimination mechanism for the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer — e.g., 'treat each finding as distinct if its `criterion` field (or first-sentence summary) differs; fall back to asserting findings count >= N if no structured field exists' — so the executor does not need to design this from scratch.
findings.finding: The scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` is described behaviorally ('assert >= N findings, each naming a different criterion') but the plan does not specify HOW the scorer determines that two findings 'name different criteria' — e.g., by checking a structured `criterion_id` field, by string-matching AC text, or by some other heuristic. An executor implementing this scorer must make a real design choice that affects whether the scorer can false-positive or false-negative, and no default is stated.
findings.location: What section: 'a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` — on a case whose fixture ticket has N independently-unmet criteria, assert the FAIL verdict emits a distinct finding for EACH (>= N findings, each naming a different criterion)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the scorer description: 'Uniqueness is determined by the `criterion_id` field of each finding (or, if absent, by the first AC label mentioned in the finding text). The scorer passes if `len({f.criterion_id for f in verdict.findings}) >= N`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether 'close path' in AC item 3 means only `reconcile_verdict` pass-through (testable via `_verify()` + FakeRunner in test_completion_verifier.py) or also the `transition_close` rendering path (requiring a monkeypatched close test); specify the exact seam the test covers.
findings.finding: The plan says the new FakeRunner unit test asserts a two-finding FAIL verdict is preserved 'through reconcile_verdict + the close path', but does not specify what 'the close path' means in a FakeRunner test context. The close path lives in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` and is exercised via `rebar.transition(…, 'closed')` with monkeypatching (as in `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py`), not via `verify_completion` + `FakeRunner` alone. The mirrored file `tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py` never exercises the close-gate rendering path. The plan is ambiguous about whether 'close path' means (a) `reconcile_verdict` pass-through only, or (b) the full `transition_close._completion_precheck` render path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer's decision rule in the plan: define (a) what case field encodes N (e.g. a new `min_findings` field on the dataset case, or derive from the fixture), and (b) the exact predicate — e.g. `verdict == FAIL AND len(findings) >= N AND len({f['criterion'] for f in findings}) >= N`.
findings.finding: The decision rule / predicate logic for the new `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer is entirely unspecified: the plan names it but never defines what the scorer function body should check (e.g., assert len(findings) >= N, assert each finding's `criterion` field is distinct, or both), leaving the implementer with no executable specification.
findings.location: What section ('a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria`') / AC line 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how N is communicated to the `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer at runtime — e.g. define a new optional `min_findings: int` field on dataset cases (added to the case schema/validator), set to `2` on `CV-two-unmet-criteria`, and document that the scorer reads `case.get('min_findings', 1)`.
findings.finding: The `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer needs to know N (the count of independently-unmet criteria for the `CV-two-unmet-criteria` case) at scoring time, but no mechanism for the case to communicate N to the scorer is specified — the dataset case schema carries no such field and the scorer signature `(case, out) -> ScoreResult` provides no other channel.
findings.location: What section (CV-two-unmet-criteria case definition) / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py (Scorer type alias line 61)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope the new FakeRunner unit test to the close-path surface not already covered by `test_op_fail_findings_each_have_criterion_and_detail`; explicitly note that the `reconcile_verdict` pass-through is already proven by that existing test to avoid a near-duplicate.
findings.finding: The proposed new FakeRunner unit test for the 'plumbing invariant' (multi-finding FAIL survives reconcile_verdict) is substantially duplicated by the existing `test_op_fail_findings_each_have_criterion_and_detail` test, which already feeds a two-finding FAIL through FakeRunner → verify_completion (which calls reconcile_verdict) and asserts both findings survive. The plan describes this as a new guard without acknowledging the overlap.
findings.location: What section: 'Additionally add a FakeRunner unit test ... asserting the PLUMBING invariant: a multi-finding FAIL verdict survives reconcile_verdict and the close path with all findings intact'; tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py lines 371-402

findings.finding: The plan's AC2 test mechanism is underspecified in a way that allows a trivially-passing FakeRunner unit test to satisfy it without actually demonstrating that the prompt change achieves exhaustive evaluation. A FakeRunner test simply returns whatever findings are pre-loaded — reconcile_verdict already passes through multi-findings by construction (existing test_op_fail_findings_each_have_criterion_and_detail already covers this shape). The eval spec (completion-verifier.eval.yaml) has no case with two independently-unmet criteria that expects findings for both. The 'eval or unit fixture' disjunction permits a test that proves nothing about prompt-level exhaustiveness.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / 'Add/extend an eval or unit fixture' (What section)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add an eval case to completion-verifier.eval.yaml, e.g.:
```yaml
- id: CV-two-unmet-criteria
  corpus: rebar
  expect: fail
  note: "Both AC items are unmet (no --json flag, no test file) → FAIL must enumerate both."
  ticket_context: |
    ## Acceptance Criteria
    - [ ] support `--json` output on `foo`
    - [ ] cover `foo` with a unit test in tests/test_foo.py
  files:
    src/cli.py: |
      def foo(args):
          print("foo")  # no --json
```
And add/extend a scorer (or a unit fixture assertion) that requires `len(findings) >= 2` for this case. The existing `_recall` scorer only checks polarity; a `finding_count_on_multi_unmet` scorer or an assertion in a test fixture (`assert len(r['findings']) >= 2`) would close the gap.

findings.finding: The proposed two-unmet-criteria fixture (AC2) lacks a scorer or assertion that verifies BOTH findings appear — the existing `recall_on_incomplete` scorer only checks `verdict == FAIL`, so the new case would pass even if the model still stops at the first failure, making the fixture vacuous against the anti-ratchet property it is meant to enforce.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a lightweight deterministic scorer to `eval_scorers.py` — e.g. `'all_criteria_enumerated': lambda case, out: ScoreResult(False, len(out.get('findings', [])) >= case.get('min_findings', 1), ...)` — and annotate the new two-unmet-criteria dataset case with `min_findings: 2`. Alternatively, add a unit test in `tests/unit/` that stubs a two-criterion ticket response and asserts `len(verdict['findings']) == 2`.

findings.finding: The plan's AC2 test/fixture can be satisfied without ever exercising the updated prompt: the existing `_recall` / `recall_on_incomplete` scorer only checks `_fired()` (verdict == FAIL, findings >= 1), and the FakeRunner path injects pre-canned findings, bypassing the prompt entirely. No scorer or assertion verifies that `len(findings) == number_of_unmet_criteria` — so a FakeRunner test with two pre-canned findings trivially passes AC2 even if the real prompt still stops at the first failure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated scorer `all_unmet_criteria_enumerated` (or extend `_schema_verdict`) that, for a fixture case tagged `expect: fail` with a `min_findings: 2` annotation, fails if `len(findings) < 2`. Alternatively, write the AC2 test using the real agentic op (not FakeRunner) against a scripted ticket with exactly two unmet ACs and assert `len(r['findings']) == 2`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
